pages.raw_text:
City Engineer Ralston, who a
few days ago gave Willett & Camp
the work of hauling rock to the
concrete mixer at the Washington
st. bridge, was severely criticized
at the council meeting last night
by J. H. Stevenson, a member of
the team owners union, who said
that the engineers action would re-
suit in the city paying more for the
work than it was before when the
team owners union was doing it
for $3.50 a day. He said that Raj
ston gave the work to Willett &
Camp for $115 a load, while Mr..
Bowers, a member of the union,
offered to take it for $1.1o a load.
A load is supposed to be two cubic
yards, which make the Willett &
Camp bid 57h cents yard. Mr..
Stevenson said the teamsters had


been hauling from six to seven
loads day for $3.50. If each of the
loads contained two cubic yards,
the cost would be about 451a cents
yard, and therefore the Ralston
deal would be expensive. But 1t is
claimed that the union team own
ers were hauling not over a yard
and half per load. If this is true.
then the Ralston deal is saving.
Mr.. Ralston was not present at the
meeting, so the matter was re-
ferred to the committee of the
whole meeting next Tuesday night.
at which time it will be discussed
and Mr.. Ralston given an oppor-
tunity to present his reasons for
making the deal. The city charter
provides that only the board OF
works shall let contracts. and that
point is also involved, but the
council has taken the bridge out
of the hands of the board and
turned it over to the city engineer.
